commands.author: samson910022

target.title: fix(opencode-go): add qwen3.7-max, qwen3.7-plus, minimax-m3 to provider catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `opencode-go/qwen3.7-max`, `opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus`, and `opencode-go/minimax-m3` are missing from the opencode-go provider's static model catalog, failing at runtime with `Unknown model ... (model_not_found)`.
- Why it matters: These models exist upstream (confirmed via `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models` and the official [OpenCode Go docs](https://opencode.ai/docs/zh-tw/go/)), but the bundled static catalog in `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts` has no entries for them — so any config or agent that references them hits `model_not_found` and falls back to other providers.
- What changed: Added three catalog entries matching the upstream model IDs, using the `anthropic-messages` API format (same pattern as the existing `minimax-m2.5` entry).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to provider routing logic, dynamic catalog behavior, or any other provider.

---

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

---

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Integrations

---

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #91238
- Related: #90088 (same class: Anthropic Haiku 4.5 missing from static catalog)
- Related: #90465 (feature request: auto-discover models from `/v1/models`)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

---

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The opencode-go provider's bundled static catalog (`provider-catalog.ts`) lists all supported models. Three models that exist upstream (`qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `minimax-m3`) were never added to the catalog. Because OpenClaw validates model IDs against the provider's catalog, any reference to these IDs fails with `model_not_found` even though the upstream API serves them.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No automated sync between the upstream OpenCode `/go/v1/models` endpoint and the bundled catalog.
- Contributing context: These models were added to the OpenCode Go service after the last catalog update; the official docs now list them but the bundled catalog was not updated in sync.

---

## Changes

- Added `minimax-m3`, `qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus` entries to `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts`
  - All three use `api: "anthropic-messages"` format (matching `minimax-m2.5`)

Model specs:

| Model ID | API | Context | Max Tokens | Cost (in/out/cacheR/cacheW) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `minimax-m3` | `anthropic-messages` | 1,000,000 | 131,072 | $0.60 / $2.40 / $0.12 / $0.75 |
| `qwen3.7-max` | `anthropic-messages` | 1,000,000 | 65,536 | $2.50 / $7.50 / $0.50 / $3.125 |
| `qwen3.7-plus` | `anthropic-messages` | 1,000,000 | 65,536 | $0.40 / $1.60 / $0.04 / $0.50 (≤256K tier) |

---

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Previously failing models now:
  - Appear in `openclaw models list --provider opencode-go`
  - Resolve correctly at runtime as `available: true, missing: false`
  - No longer trigger unexpected fallback to other providers

---

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Three opencode-go models (`qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `minimax-m3`) resolve at runtime instead of failing with `model_not_found`.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15 (arm64), Node v26.0.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (stable). `OPENCODE_API_KEY` configured and set. Models declared in `agents.defaults.models`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```
  1. Apply catalog patch to provider-catalog-CTCbBYfT.js
  2. Restart gateway: openclaw gateway restart
  3. Run: openclaw models list --provider opencode-go --json --all
  ```

- **Before evidence:**

  ```
  00:04:17 [model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision:
    decision=candidate_failed
    requested=opencode-go/qwen3.7-max
    reason=model_not_found
    detail=Unknown model: opencode-go/qwen3.7-max.
    Found agents.defaults.models["opencode-go/qwen3.7-max"],
    but no matching models.providers["opencode-go"].models[] entry.

  00:05:32 [model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision:
    decision=candidate_failed
    requested=opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus
    reason=model_not_found
    detail=Unknown model: opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus.
    ...

  00:08:28 [model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision:
    decision=candidate_failed
    requested=opencode-go/minimax-m3
    reason=model_not_found
    detail=Unknown model: opencode-go/minimax-m3.
    ...
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  ```
  $ openclaw models list --provider opencode-go --json --all
  ...
  opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus  → available: true, missing: false, contextWindow: 1,000,000
  opencode-go/qwen3.7-max   → available: true, missing: false, contextWindow: 1,000,000
  opencode-go/minimax-m3    → available: true, missing: false, contextWindow: 1,000,000
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** All three models resolve with `available: true` and `missing: false`. Gateway processes inbound messages successfully without `model_not_found` errors. Runtime log after restart shows normal agent operation.

- **What was not tested:** Full end-to-end inference through each of the three new models. The fix is validated at the model resolution level — the same pattern used by all other catalog entries in this provider.

---

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

---

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

---

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Static catalog may drift from upstream OpenCode Go model list.
- Mitigation: Same pattern used by all other bundled catalogs in the codebase. The upstream models endpoint (`/go/v1/models`) confirms these IDs exist and are actively served.
